title: Boolean expressions should not be compared to a boolean literal
messageFormat: Use the boolean expression directly instead of comparing it to 'true' or 'false'.
summary: Comparing a boolean value to true or false adds a layer that says nothing: x == true is just x, and x == false is just !x. The literal comparison only lengthens the expression.
Dropping the comparison reads closer to the condition's intent and removes a classic spot where a stray single = turns a test into an assignment.
